statement.p: Currently, an artist can sell 240 paintings every year at the price of {\$90.00} per painting. Each time he raises the price per painting by {\$15.00}, he sells 5 fewer paintings every year.

li.p: To obtain maximum income of , the artist should set the price per painting at .
To earn {\$43{,}875.00} per year, the artist could sell his paintings at two different prices. The lower price is per painting, and the higher price is per painting.

solution.p: Assume the artist will raise the price per painting x times.
The current price per painting is {\$90.00}. After raising the price x times, each time by {\$15.00}, the new price per painting will become 90+15x dollars.

Currently he sells 240 paintings per year. Its given that he will sell 5 fewer paintings each time he raises the price. After raising the price per painting x times, he will sell 240-5x paintings every year.

The artists income can be calculated by multiplying the number of paintings sold with price per painting. If he raises the price per painting x times, his new yearly income can be modeled by the expression:
which gives his yearly income in dollars.
We can write this quadratic expression in standard form:
Written in standard form, we can see:

The graph of y = -75x^2+3150x+21600 is an upside-down parabola. The maximum income he can make is represented by the parabolas vertex. To locate its vertex, we first find its axis by the axis formula:
Next, we substitute x=21 into the expression to find its vertexs y-value:

The vertex is at {\left(21,54675\right)}. This implies the artist can earn a maximum yearly income of {\$54{,}675.00}. This happens if he increases the price per painting 21 times, making the new price 90+21\cdot15=405 dollars.
If the artist wants to make {\$43{,}875.00} per year, we will set the expression representing income equal to 43875, and we have:
Next, we can solve this quadratic equation with the Quadratic Formula. Identify that a=-75,b=3150,c=-22275.
If the artist raises the price 9 times, the new price would be 90+15\cdot9=225 dollars per painting.
If the artist raises the price 33 times, the new price would be 90+15\cdot33=585 dollars per painting.
To earn {\$43{,}875.00} per year, the artist could sell his paintings at two different prices. The lower price is {\$225.00} per painting, and the higher price is {\$585.00} dollars per painting.
The following graph shows the points representing the solutions above:
